signals.title: NZD/USD — G10 Bilateral Theme + Fed/RBNZ Divergence
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5634, weakening for the eighth consecutive day. USD is the strongest G10 currency this week (+0.907% vs 7 peers) while NZD is the weakest (−1.661% vs 4 peers), creating a 2.568% leader-to-laggard spread. This divergence is driven by growing Fed hike bets (US PCE > 4%, Williams sees 2% inflation only in 2028) against a mildly accommodative RBNZ real rate (−0.83%). NZD COT LF net short −22,454 (extreme 18.4% long%) vs RBNZ hike bias (OIS pricing a hike for next meeting) creates short-squeeze risk if RBNZ delivers. However, retail is 93% long, indicating extreme contrarian long positioning. Watch for RBNZ's next meeting on 8 Jul for a catalyst.

signals.title: USD/JPY — BoJ Hawkish Shift + Intervention Risk + Carry
signals.text: USD/JPY at 161.79, consolidating near 40-year lows. Tokyo Core CPI rose to 1.6% and core-core CPI hit 1.9% (above forecast), signaling firming underlying inflation and a hawkish shift from the BoJ. This increases the likelihood of a BoJ policy adjustment, challenging the 264bp USD-JPY OIS carry advantage. JPY COT LF net short −112,092 (29.7% long%) is a crowded carry trade, vulnerable to a short-squeeze on BoJ action. Intervention risk remains elevated on a sustained break above 160.00. The next BoJ meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 31 Jul is a key catalyst.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Volatility + CESI
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.6885, edging lower on Fed hike bets. Options market pricing elevated protection with IV 16.3% vs HV30 8.1% (VRP +8.2%), indicating strong hedging demand. AUD COT LF net long +38,548 (70.2% long%) is a significant speculative long position. This is compounded by a moderate negative CESI for AUD (net −7 misses), suggesting data is disappointing expectations. The combination of crowded long positioning, elevated vol premium, and negative data surprises points to downside risk. Watch for further Fed rhetoric and upcoming RBA commentary.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Correlation Break + Retail Divergence
signals.text: USD/CAD at 1.4202. The correlation between USD/CAD and WTI Oil has broken significantly, with corr=−0.694 vs 252d norm=+0.027 (z=−2.15σ). This indicates CAD is not benefiting from oil price movements as it historically would, suggesting other factors are dominating. Retail sentiment shows an extreme 93% short bias, which is a contrarian signal for reversal. However, CAD CESI is moderately positive (+9 beats), supporting CAD strength. The next BoC meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 15 Jul will clarify policy direction.
